description_long: This is a modern military fighter jet with a sleek, aerodynamic fuselage and a camouflage paint scheme featuring green, brown, and tan patches. The aircraft has sharply swept-back wings with small wingtip extensions and a single vertical stabilizer at the tail. The nose is pointed and painted dark gray, housing a forward-positioned, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy. The jet engine is mounted at the rear, with a prominent exhaust nozzle visible from behind. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it is likely retractable and currently stowed. The overall design is optimized for high-speed flight and maneuverability, typical of combat aircraft.
description: A modern military fighter jet with a camouflage pattern, swept-back wings, and a single rear jet engine. It features a pointed nose, enclosed cockpit, and a single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, camouflage pattern, and the pointed nose with the cockpit canopy visible.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the sleek fuselage, swept-back wings, and the forward-positioned cockpit with the jet engine at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the single vertical stabilizer, engine exhaust nozzle, and the symmetry of the wings and tail surfaces.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile mirroring view 1, showing the same aerodynamic features and camouflage pattern.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with sharply swept-back wings and small wingtip extensions; no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single rear-mounted jet engine with visible exhaust nozzle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear (not visible, inferred from typical design and lack of visible gear).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy integrated into the fuselage.
